author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
jñānī jñānayoge, kariyā jatane,
brahmera sākṣāt kare
brahma⋅sākṣātkāra, aprārabdha karma,
sampūrṇa jñānete hare

tabu ta’ prārabdha, nāhi haya kṣaya,
phalabhoga vinā kabhu
brahmabhūta jīva, phalabhoga lāgi’,
janama⋅maraṇa labhu

kintu ohe nāma, tava sphūrti ha’le,
ekāntī janera āra
prārabdhāprārabdha, kichu nāhi thāke,
vede gāya bāra bāra

tomāra udaye, jīvera hṛdaya,
sampūrṇa śodhita haya
karmajñāna⋅bandha, saba dūre jāya,
anāyāse bhava⋅kṣaya

bhakativinoda, bāhu tule kaya,
nāmera niśāna dhara
nāmaḍaṅkā⋅dhvani, kariyā jāibe,
bheṭibe muralīdhara
en_translation:
A wise person, through the path of knowledge and with great effort, realizes the Absolute Truth. This realization completely removes the accumulated reactions to past actions through perfect knowledge.

Even then, the results of current actions are not destroyed; they must still be experienced. A soul absorbed in that Absolute Truth must take birth and death to experience those results.

But, O Name! When You manifest, for a one-pointed devotee, all karmic reactions cease to exist. The Vedas sing this again and again.

With Your appearance, the heart of the soul becomes completely purified. All the bondage of action and knowledge goes far away, and material existence is destroyed effortlessly.

Bhaktivinoda raises his arms and says: "Hold the banner of the Name! By making the sound of the Holy Name resound, you will meet the flute-player!"
